exemplar.title:
To what extent do metal ions (silver, copper, barium, and zinc)
affect the inhibition of Lactobacillus acidophilus growth at different concentrations
(1 M, 0.1 M, 0.01M, 0.001 M) in 24 hours as measured by the zone of inhibition?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent do metal ions (silver, copper, barium, and zinc)
affect the inhibition of Lactobacillus acidophilus growth at different concentrations
(1 M, 0.1 M, 0.01M, 0.001 M) in 24 hours as measured by the zone of inhibition?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear, accurate and effective communication of the global problem and underlying mechanisms
Explicit, sharply delimited research question maintained throughout
Comprehensive, replicable methodology with justified selection of materials and controls
weaknesses: Minor phrasing lapses in ROS generation mechanism
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this investigation firmly in the top band (5â6). The topic is communicated with comprehensive explanation; the research question is focused and appropriate; and the methodology is fully detailed and justified. Minor suggestions for clarity do not undermine the consistently high performance across all subâstrands.

strengths: Broad and relevant use of over twenty scholarly sources
Accurate and consistent use of scientific terminology and notation
weaknesses: Occasional notation slips (e.g., Agâ  vs Agâº)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although a more explicit integration of quantitative data from sources would strengthen the background, the student applies source material coherently to justify hypotheses and interpret anomalies, and uses terminology accurately and consistently. This aligns with the top band descriptor for clear, coherent knowledge and effective use of sources.

strengths: Thorough data analysis with averages, standard deviations, percentage deviations and two-way ANOVA
Well-supported discussion of trends, comparison with literature, and methodological limitations
Logical and coherent argument structure from hypothesis to conclusion
weaknesses: Some speculative claims (e.g., wound dressing biocompatibility) without direct evidence
Minor statistical reporting issues (p-values, handling of zero-value deviations)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates excellent research analysis that remains consistently focused on the research question, a well-structured and coherent argument, and strong critical evaluation of limitations and extensions. Minor issues in statistical reporting and occasional speculative conclusions prevent a perfect 12, but performance clearly sits at the upper end of the critical thinking rubric.

strengths: Clear and appropriate overall structure with complete table of contents and labeled sections
Consistent layout, correct formatting of tables, figures, equations, and page numbering
weaknesses: Truncated table content on one page
Image captions lack scale bars or magnification details
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation fully meets the criteria for the top band (3â4). The reportâs organization, formatting conventions, and internal navigation are handled expertly. Minor truncation and missing scale bars are easily correctable and do not detract from an otherwise flawless presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report contains title page, abstract substitute, table of contents, labelled sections, tables, figures and bibliography in conventional EE order.  Transitions are smooth and internal headings match the TOC, providing a clear and appropriate structure throughout, warranting the top mark of this band.

Page numbers, consistent font sizing, properly bordered tables and correctly captioned images are present.  Scientific units and equation formatting are generally correct.  Minor inconsistencies in sub-superscript formatting and occasional truncated URLs exist but overall layout and formatting conventions are met accurately.

The essay follows a logical flow from introduction to conclusion, with clear sign-posting via headings and tables.  Arguments build cumulatively: hypothesis â data â statistical test â interpretation.  Minor inconsistencies (e.g., copper expected > silver) are acknowledged but not fully reconciled.  Overall coherence and structure align with the lower end of the 7-9 band.

The student introduces the global problem of antimicrobial resistance, explains the oligodynamic effect, and links this directly to the purpose of the investigation.  Key biological terms (MIC, ROS, Gram-positive) are defined in context, and the rationale for selecting each metal and the bacterium is clearly articulated.  The explanation is consistently accurate and sufficiently detailed to give a reader unfamiliar with the topic a comprehensive understanding.  Minor lapses in phrasing (e.g., confusing ROS generation wording) do not detract from an otherwise strong and coherent communication of the topic.

Over twenty scholarly sources are cited and are mostly relevant to metal toxicity mechanisms or laboratory safety.  Source material is applied to justify predictions and to interpret anomalies (silver vs copper).  However, much of the background remains descriptive and occasionally repetitive, and direct integration of primary data from the cited studies into the studentâs argument is limited.  This aligns with competent but partially effective application.

The student processes raw data into averages, standard deviations, percentage deviations, and employs a two-way ANOVA to test three null hypotheses, demonstrating sound analytical technique.  Results are interpreted in light of statistical significance and linked back to MIC determination.  Analysis remains clearly relevant to the research question, though mechanistic depth could be richer, positioning the work solidly in the 7-9 band with a mid-high score.

A full, replicable procedure is presented, including sterilisation steps, controls, five repeats, quantitative measurements, and statistical treatment (two-way ANOVA).  Controlled-variable tables and risk/ethical sections show deliberate selection and justification of methods.  The range of primary and secondary sources (mechanistic literature, MSDS documents, statistical tools) demonstrates thoughtful methodological planning consistent with the highest band.

The discussion explains trends, compares findings with literature, and evaluates experimental limitations (temperature control, tablet impurities, measurement precision).  Suggestions for improvement and extensions are provided.  While conclusions are generally supported by data, some claims (toxicity implications for wound dressings) are speculative and not fully evidenced, so the evaluation is good rather than excellent, meriting the top of the 4-6 band.

The research question is stated explicitly, is sharply delimited to four specific metal ions, four concentrations, a fixed incubation time and a single quantitative response variable.  Throughout the report the student retains this focus, returning to it in the hypothesis, data analysis and conclusion sections.  The scope is entirely appropriate for a Biology EE and is neither overly broad nor trivial, satisfying the descriptor for the top band.

Scientific terminology (oligodynamic effect, CFU, two-way ANOVA, standard deviation) is used accurately and consistently.  Equations are formatted correctly, units are stated, and symbols are appropriate.  Occasional notational slips (e.g., Agâ ) are minor and do not impede clarity, meeting the descriptor for accurate and consistent use.

annotations.comment: The claim that silver and copper could be used in wound dressings âwithout causing harmâ is speculative. Cite biocompatibility studies or discuss cytotoxicity to support this recommendation.
Reporting p-values as exactly 0 is misleading. Instead, state p < 0.001 or the precise value to reflect statistical reality and maintain scientific rigour.

The inclusion of the research question at the start demonstrates clear focus and exceptional alignment with the investigationâs aims. It is concise, specific to four metal ions, and measurable via inhibition zones, showing excellent formulation and focus.
The title is clear and informative, accurately reflecting the subject and design of the study. It effectively sets reader expectations and adheres to IB formatting requirements.
Excellent adherence to the word-count limit (3788 words). Including the word count here meets the formal requirement and ensures compliance with IB guidelines.
The table of contents is well organised with all major sections and sub-headings clearly listed. This structure aids navigation and reflects strong presentation skills.

Background on antibiotic resistance is descriptive, but the integration of specific data from cited studies is limited. To deepen understanding, explicitly link quantitative findings from your references to your context.
The definition of the oligodynamic effect is accurate. To strengthen critical depth, illustrate with a concise example (e.g., specific MIC values) from one of your sources.
The mechanism of ROS generation by silver ions is described, but the phrasing âindirectly generate ROSâ is vague. Specify the chemical reactions or intermediates involved for clarity.
Footnote formatting is inconsistent (e.g., use of â&sup>â). Ensure a uniform citation style (superscript numbers without HTML artifacts) throughout the document.
Image captions are present, but the micrographs lack scale bars. Include scale bars on all images or state magnification so readers can assess dimensions accurately.
The notation âCu^{2}â in your caption omits the superscript plus sign. Consistently use correct ion notation (e.g., Cu^{2+}) to avoid confusion.
The description of zincâs oxidative stress effect states âmoderateâ without quantification. Consider adding comparative data or values from your sources to contextualize âmoderate.â
Literature review summarises previous MIC studies but lacks a clear gap statement. Explicitly state what remains unknown and how your experiment addresses it.
Your variables table combines both metal type and concentration under one independent variable heading. For clarity, separate metal identity and concentration into distinct independent variables.
Table 2 (controlled variables) appears blank on page 10âensure the table content is visible on the PDF or reflow your layout so it does not truncate.
In your written hypothesis you use Ag^{\dagger} instead of Ag^{+}, which is nonstandard. Ensure you use accurate chemical notation to maintain conceptual clarity.
The procedure omits pH monitoring of the agar medium, which can influence bacterial growth. To strengthen methodological control, record and report agar pH.
Using a light-bulb-heated box instead of an incubator introduces unquantified temperature variations. Include temperature logs or data to support reliability of your incubation method.
The qualitative rating using â+++++â lacks quantitative detail. Consider presenting average inhibition diameters alongside or instead of symbolic ratings for precise analysis.
In Table 4 the symbols \overline{2} and \tau appear without definition. Provide clear legends or footnotes for all mathematical symbols to aid reader understanding.
Your formula for average area divides by 4, but you conducted five trials. Adjust the denominator to 5 to correctly calculate the mean area.
Percentage deviation is applied to zero-value inhibition zones (0 mm) which yields undefined results. Discuss how you handle divisions by zero or omit these points from percentage calculations.
Graph 1 caption ends mid-sentence (âabove andâ). Complete the description of error bars and explicitly state what they represent for clarity.
The evaluation acknowledges temperature as a limitation but omits other factors like humidity and pH. Broaden reflection to include these variables for a more comprehensive discussion.
Recommending digital calipers is an excellent methodological improvement. This specific suggestion will significantly reduce measurement error and enhance data reliability.
The conclusion reiterates background rather than synthesizing findings. Focus your conclusion on answering the research question directly and highlighting the significance of your results.
Bibliography is extensive, but citations sometimes lack consistent formatting (italics, punctuation). Adopt a uniform citation style (APA/MLA) for all entries.
Page numbering appears consistently in footers from page 2 onwardâthis meets the formal requirement and aids navigation.
